concerns.text: The plan cites DOI 10.1038/sdata.2017.85 (Gaultois et al., 'Data from: A database of thermoelectric materials') as the sole source. This dataset primarily contains bulk thermoelectric properties (Seebeck, conductivity, ZT) but lacks the explicit, structured 'elemental composition' breakdown (e.g., stoichiometric coefficients per element) required to calculate the proposed features (mean atomic radius, VEC, electronegativity variance) for arbitrary alloys. The plan assumes the raw data contains 'elemental composition' or a parsable formula string for every record, which is not guaranteed by the dataset's schema. If the dataset only provides material names (e.g., 'Bi2Te3') without parsed element lists, the feature engineering step (FR-003) will fail or require an external, unverified chemical formula parser not cited in the plan. The plan must verify the dataset schema supports direct extraction of constituent elements and stoichiometry or cite a supplementary dataset that provides this granularity.

The plan states the system 'filters for specific material families (Bismuth Telluride, Lead Telluride, Skutterudites)'. The cited dataset (DOI 10.1038/sdata.2017.85) is a heterogeneous collection of thousands of materials. The plan does not cite a source or method for reliably identifying 'Skutterudites' or distinguishing specific alloy families within the raw data solely by string matching, as the dataset may use inconsistent naming conventions (e.g., 'CoSb3' vs 'Skutterudite'). Without a verified mapping or a dataset that explicitly tags material families, the filtering logic (FR-002) is brittle and may exclude valid data or include noise, invalidating the statistical analysis.
